summary: Disables NetBIOS over TCP/IP through the documented per-interface Windows control surface. Official Microsoft documentation exposes NetbiosOptions per adapter with values 0 = DHCP setting, 1 = enabled, 2 = disabled, and also documents the Win32_NetworkAdapterConfiguration.SetTcpipNetbios method for applying that state. The app now uses a command-backed per-interface implementation that sets NetbiosOptions = 2 on all IP-enabled adapters and can restore the previously detected per-adapter state.

setting.name: NetBIOS over TCP/IP
setting.casual_explanation: NetBIOS over TCP/IP is an older naming and networking layer that some legacy Windows networks still use. Most modern home users never notice it unless they have very old devices or legacy enterprise naming dependencies.
setting.why_people_change_it: People disable it to modernize the network stack and reduce reliance on old name-resolution behavior. They keep it enabled only when old devices or legacy discovery workflows still depend on it.
setting.tradeoffs_overview: Disabling NetBIOS over TCP/IP is usually harmless on modern networks, but some old discovery and share-browsing workflows can break. This tweak now uses the documented per-adapter Windows control rather than an unrelated global NetBT parameter.
setting.professional_notes: Windows exposes NetBIOS over TCP/IP control per adapter through NetbiosOptions and the Win32_NetworkAdapterConfiguration.SetTcpipNetbios method. The app now uses a command-backed tweak to enumerate IP-enabled adapters and apply SetTcpipNetbios(2), which corresponds to NetbiosOptions = 2 (disabled) for each adapter. This is more accurate than the earlier NoNameReleaseOnDemand write under the global NetBT parameters key, which affected name release behavior rather than the actual NetBIOS over TCP/IP mode switch.

allowed_values.label: Use DHCP setting
allowed_values.meaning: Windows follows the NetBIOS preference provided by DHCP for that adapter.

allowed_values.label: Enabled
allowed_values.meaning: NetBIOS over TCP/IP is enabled for that adapter.

allowed_values.label: Disabled
allowed_values.meaning: NetBIOS over TCP/IP is disabled for that adapter.

targets.notes: This is adapter-specific. A correct app experience may need to enumerate interfaces instead of pretending the setting is a single global toggle.

app_current_implementation.notes: The app now uses the documented per-interface control model. NetworkTweakProvider exposes the tweak card and delegates apply/verify/rollback to a command-backed tweak that enumerates IP-enabled adapters and applies Win32_NetworkAdapterConfiguration.SetTcpipNetbios(2).

windows_defaults.label: Per-adapter Windows baseline

states.rationale: The documented baseline is the DHCP-controlled option.

windows_defaults.notes: This is not a single device-wide value. A correct restore-default flow needs to understand interface scope.

recommended_profiles.label: Windows default
intended_for: Networks where NetBIOS needs are unknown
avoid_for: People who have already verified that NetBIOS must be disabled per adapter

states.rationale: Keeps adapter behavior under the documented DHCP-controlled default.

recommended_profiles.good_when: You want the documented Windows baseline without pretending there is one magic global switch.
recommended_profiles.bad_when: You need a deliberate per-interface hard-disable.
tradeoffs: Behavior can still vary by adapter and DHCP configuration.
This is more honest than an incorrect global toggle.

recommended_profiles.label: Modern TCP/IP only
intended_for: Users who want to remove legacy NetBIOS dependencies
avoid_for: Old NAS or printer discovery scenarios

states.rationale: Candidate recommended value for disabling NetBIOS over TCP/IP per adapter.

recommended_profiles.good_when: You have confirmed that the adapters and devices involved do not need NetBIOS.
recommended_profiles.bad_when: You still depend on legacy browsing or name-resolution paths.
tradeoffs: Legacy discovery workflows may stop working.
This profile is documented but not approved for Apply until the app models per-interface scope correctly.
recommended_profiles.notes: Applies NetbiosOptions = 2 per IP-enabled adapter through the documented WMI method and records prior per-adapter state for rollback.

recommended_profiles.label: Legacy NetBIOS required

states.rationale: Candidate recommended value for explicitly enabling NetBIOS over TCP/IP per adapter.

recommended_profiles.good_when: A required legacy workflow is confirmed to depend on NetBIOS.
recommended_profiles.bad_when: You are enabling it without proof that you need it.
tradeoffs: Compatibility improves for some old workflows.
This profile is documented but not approved for Apply until scope and UX are redesigned.
recommended_profiles.notes: Candidate profile only. Do not expose as a device-wide Apply preset yet.

evidence.title: Microsoft WMI: SetTcpipNetbios method
evidence.summary: Official documentation for the per-adapter NetBIOS over TCP/IP control values 0, 1, and 2.

evidence.title: Microsoft unattended setup: NetbiosOptions
evidence.summary: Official documentation for the NetbiosOptions values used per interface.

evidence.title: Current app implementation
evidence.summary: NetworkTweakProvider exposes the netbios tweak and delegates it to a command-backed per-interface implementation.

evidence.title: Command-backed NetBIOS implementation
evidence.summary: The engine tweak enumerates IP-enabled adapters, detects current TcpipNetbiosOptions state, applies Win32_NetworkAdapterConfiguration.SetTcpipNetbios(2), verifies that all targeted adapters report 2, and can restore the previously detected per-adapter state during rollback.

evidence.title: Local read-only CIM detect run for NetBIOS over TCP/IP
evidence.summary: Read-only Get-CimInstance Win32_NetworkAdapterConfiguration run on the current machine returned four IP-enabled adapters and exposed TcpipNetbiosOptions for each, confirming that the documented detect surface is live on this build.

evidence.title: Existing tweak provenance record
evidence.summary: Repo provenance links the old tweak history to NoNameReleaseOnDemand references. This remains useful as audit history, but the live implementation now follows the documented NetbiosOptions and SetTcpipNetbios surfaces instead.

decision.why: Official Microsoft documentation is explicit about the real per-interface control surface: NetbiosOptions values 0, 1, and 2 plus the SetTcpipNetbios method. The app now matches that model with a command-backed implementation that enumerates IP-enabled adapters, applies the documented disabled state, and keeps prior per-adapter state for rollback.

open_questions: Should the UI eventually expose adapter selection instead of applying the documented disabled state to all IP-enabled adapters?

validation_proof.exact_quote_or_path: SetTcpipNetbios method of the Win32_NetworkAdapterConfiguration class. The SetTcpipNetbios method sets the TCP/IP NetBIOS options for the network adapter. Option 0 = Use NetBIOS setting from the DHCP server. Option 1 = Enable NetBIOS. Option 2 = Disable NetBIOS.
validation_proof.notes: Microsoft's official WMI page and unattended setup page both document the same per-interface 0/1/2 NetBIOS control. The app now matches that model with a command-backed SetTcpipNetbios(2) implementation rather than the old global NoNameReleaseOnDemand write.
